title: Import Pages Request Schema
description: Schema for validating/importing a portable page bundle (issue #30, Phase 5). Used by both /admin/pages/import/validate and /admin/pages/import.

bundle.description: The exported page bundle (as produced by /admin/pages/export)

format.description: Bundle format identifier written by the exporter, e.g. 'selfhelp/page-bundle'.

version.description: Bundle format version written by the exporter, e.g. '1.0'.

bundle_version.description: Deprecated legacy alias for 'version'; tolerated for older hand-written bundles. Prefer 'version'.

data_tables.description: Optional data-table definitions (and optional sample rows) the bundle owns, keyed to their owning form section by 'owner_section_name'. Present only when the export included data tables.

owner_section_name.description: Bundle name of the form section that owns this table; relinked to the new section id on import.

columns.description: Human column names of the table (display names of the form inputs).

rows.description: Sample rows keyed by human column name; re-inserted through the form-save path when importData is true.

keywordPrefix.description: Prefix prepended to every imported page keyword (and parent references) to avoid collisions

routePrefix.description: Prefix prepended to every imported route path_pattern

skipConflictingRoutes.description: When true, routes that collide with existing active routes are skipped instead of aborting the import

activateRoutes.description: When false, imported routes are created inactive (default true)

importData.description: When true AND the bundle carries 'data_tables', the owned data tables are recreated (columns always; sample rows only when the bundle includes them). When false, only the page structure is imported and entry styles bind to an empty/existing table. Default false.

accessGroups.description: Optional group ids granted access to every imported page so real users can see them. Admin always gets full access regardless. These groups get read-only access on public pages and full CRUD on cms-app pages (surface-appropriate). Omit/empty to import admin-only.
